channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I think about building mobile apps in 2026
item.description: There are a lot of opinions about how mobile apps should be built. Frameworks, architectures, best practices… everyone has a take. But over time, I realized something: Building apps is less about...

item.title: 8 Ways to Optimize Gas Fees on Token Swaps
item.description: Gas fees are the hidden tax on every token swap. A simple ETH-to-USDC trade on Ethereum mainnet can cost $5-50 in gas depending on network congestion, while the same swap on an L2 like Arbitrum or...

item.title: 5 Swap APIs That Support Base Chain in 2026
item.description: Base has become one of the fastest-growing Layer 2 networks since Coinbase launched it in August 2023. With over $11 billion in TVL and monthly DEX volumes regularly exceeding $20 billion, Base is...

item.title: How to Build a DCA Bot with a Swap API
item.description: Dollar-cost averaging (DCA) is the practice of investing a fixed amount at regular intervals regardless of price. It is one of the most studied strategies in both traditional and crypto markets. A...

item.title: 7 Mistakes Developers Make When Integrating DEX Swaps
item.description: DEX swap volume crossed $250 billion per month in early 2026, and more of that volume is being routed programmatically through aggregator APIs. But integrating a swap API is not the same as...

item.title: Top 10 EVM Chains for Token Swaps in 2026
item.description: Choosing the right EVM chain for token swaps depends on three things: liquidity depth, transaction costs, and settlement speed. With DEX monthly volume averaging over $400 billion and total DeFi...

item.title: Atomicity - Design a Reliable Wallet Transfer System with ACID Guarantees
item.description: hi! to Design a transaction that transfers money from one account to another using the accounts table, CREATE TABLE accounts (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,name TEXT NOT NULL,balance INT NOT NULL CHECK...

item.title: Sudo (CVE-2021-3156) - I went looking for a CVE exploit and found a different bug instead, which got patched.
item.description: As i'm migrating from old Diary, i've decided to repost just a few selected post that i consider worth keeping. Not for the quality of the post, but for the content. I try to reformat it to dev.to...

item.title: Perpetual Trading Bots with Drift and Hyperliquid
item.description: Your arbitrage bot spotted a 2% price difference between Solana and Ethereum. The opportunity window is maybe 30 seconds before other bots close the gap. But your current setup needs to manage...

item.title: How We Handled 100K Items in a React List
item.description: Imagine you're building a bulk configuration UI. Users connect to a data source — a Postgres database, a SaaS API, a data warehouse — and need to select which items to include: tables, fields,...

item.title: The best deals we’ve found from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale (so far)
item.description: Amazon loves to manufacture an event. March is historically a dry spell for deals; however, with Amazon's third annual Big Spring Sale, which runs through March 31st, the retail behemoth is hoping...

item.title: Sora’s shutdown could be a reality check moment for AI video
item.description: Is this just normal corporate strategy, or are we about to see a broader pullback on AI-generated video?

item.title: Can my favorite Game Boy gadget tell fake cartridges from real?
item.description: The $50 Epilogue GB Operator has a brand-new trick up its sleeve. In addition to backing up Game Boy, Game Boy Color and Game Boy Advance cartridges and saves to a PC, it can now plug into your...

item.title: There is no ethical consumption of HBO’s Harry Potter series
item.description: In the coming years, HBO wants its new Harry Potter series to become "the streaming event of the decade" as it adapts each of the franchise's seven original books. The show could very well become...

item.title: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale is a great time to get a fancy, AI-powered bird feeder
item.description: Bird feeders have come a long way since their inception in the early 1900s. These days, it’s not all that surprising to see feeders kitted out with cameras and solar roofs, two marquee features...

item.title: The Macintosh changed computers forever
item.description: Apple's most legendary computer has two legacies: there's the computer itself, and there's the commercial. That commercial. Only a couple of days before Steve Jobs debuted the computer that would...

item.title: Apple’s long, bitter App Store antitrust war
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the legal travails of Big Tech, follow Adi Robertson. The Stepback arrives in our...

item.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
item.description: Bluesky’s new app Attie uses AI to help people build custom feeds the open social networking protocol atproto.

item.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
item.description: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.

item.title: Suno leans into customization with v5.5
item.description: Suno just released one of its biggest updates yet with v5.5 of its AI music model. Where previous updates focused mostly on improving fidelity and creating more natural vocals, v5.5 is about...

item.title: Elon Musk’s last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
item.description: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Breach FBI Director’s Personal Email, Hit Stryker With Wiper Attack
item.description: Threat actors with ties to Iran successfully broke into the personal email account of Kash Patel, the director of the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), and leaked a cache of photos and...

item.title: SteelSeries’ feature-packed Nova Pro Wireless headset is $80 off
item.description: The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless came out almost four years ago, yet it’s still one of the best gaming headsets you can buy. Turns out, it’s not easy for other companies to duplicate all...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
item.description: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell...

item.title: Why can’t TikTok identify AI generated ads when I can?
item.description: I've been struggling to tell whether the ads appearing in my TikTok feeds have been made with generative AI tools. As someone who spends a great deal of time scrutinizing images and videos for the...

item.title: Meta’s legal defeat could be a victory for children, or a loss for everyone
item.description: Is social media not just bad, but illegally bad? Should tech companies pay for making it that way? According to two US juries - and no shortage of outside commentary - the answer to both questions...

item.title: Citrix NetScaler Under Active Recon for CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS 9.3) Memory Overread Bug
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway is witnessing active reconnaissance activity, according to Defused Cyber and watchTowr. The...

item.title: A woman’s uterus has been kept alive outside the body for the first time
item.description: “Think of this as a human body,” says Javier González. In front of me is essentially a metal box on wheels. Standing at around a meter in height, it reminds me of a stainless-steel counter in a...

item.title: TA446 Deploys DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit in Targeted Spear-Phishing Campaign
item.description: Proofpoint has disclosed details of a targeted email campaign in which threat actors with ties to Russia are leveraging the recently disclosed DarkSword exploit kit to target iOS devices. The...

item.title: CISA Adds CVE-2025-53521 to KEV After Active F5 BIG-IP APM Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added a critical security flaw impacting F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager (APM) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities...
